mnemonics.title: Reasons for Process Cooperation - ICMC
mnemonics.content: "I Can Manage Conveniently" -- Information sharing, Computation speedup, Modularity, Convenience. These are the four reasons why processes cooperate in an operating system.

mnemonics.title: Shared Memory vs Message Passing - SaFe vs MeSsy
mnemonics.content: Shared memory is SaFe (Speed and Fast for large data, programmer handles Synchronization). Message passing is MeSsy (Managed by kernel, easy for Small data, good for distributed Systems). Remember: Shared = Speed but Sync yourself; Message = Managed but slower.

mnemonics.title: Bounded Buffer Conditions - FIO
mnemonics.content: Full: (In + 1) % N == Out. Empty: In == Out. Remember FIO -- Full means In is one step behind Out (circularly). Items stored = N - 1 (one wasted).
